vulnerablePopulations: rural populations with limited specialist access
epidemiology.diagnosticCriteria: Inflammatory back pain and ASAS criteria; imaging for sacroiliitis (X-ray/MRI) where available; HLA-B27 supportive

publicHealthSystem.controlRate: Low to moderate due to delayed diagnosis and limited biologic access

regionalSpecificities.diagnosisCriteria: MRI access concentrated in urban centers; diagnosis often delayed
regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: Exercise and NSAIDs; biologics for refractory disease mainly in tertiary/urban private settings; physiotherapy access variable
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: Specialist scarcity, cost barriers for biologics, and limited rehab services
regionalSpecificities.strongPoints: Affordable NSAIDs and growing tertiary rheumatology services
